mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession risk, causing investors to price in lower earnings and produce negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real sales and housing starts indicate synchronized contraction across consumer and housing demand channels, forecasting negative quarterly equity returns due to expected earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression, or joint declines fail to predict negative returns in more than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized during high inflation periods demonstrates supply-side capacity expansion that offsets cost pressures, supporting corporate margins and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns fail to materialize in more than 60 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy conditions compress equity multiples and increase borrowing costs for corporations, driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling nonfarm payrolls indicates underlying labor market fragility and data revision risk, signaling economic weakness that produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows zero predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns dominate triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5 percent during economic expansion phases signals robust aggregate demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns fail to materialize in more than 60 percent of expansion-phase observations, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks or sudden demand destruction, triggering negative short-term equity returns as markets price in operational disruptions.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 40 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.
